stem: A cylindrical pipe has an outer diameter of 10 inches and an inner diameter of 8 inches. If the pipe is 24 inches long, what is the volume of the material that makes up the pipe, in cubic inches?
explanation: Outer radius is $5$, inner radius is $4$. Outer cylinder volume: $\pi(5)^2(24) = 600\pi$. Inner cylinder volume: $\pi(4)^2(24) = 384\pi$. Material volume: $600\pi - 384\pi = 216\pi$ cubic inches. The answer is A.

distractorRationale.B: Computes only the outer cylinder volume without subtracting the inner: $\pi(5)^2(24) = 600\pi$.
distractorRationale.C: Computes only the inner cylinder volume: $\pi(4)^2(24) = 384\pi$.
distractorRationale.D: Uses the diameters instead of the radii: $\pi(10)^2(24) - \pi(8)^2(24) = 2400\pi - 1536\pi = 864\pi$.
